passages.text: The concept of sustainable development has gained significant traction in recent years. (8) ----, many corporations are now integrating environmental goals into their core strategies. This shift is not merely (9) ----; it stems from a realization that long-term profitability depends on the health of the planet. (10) ---- such initiatives, some critics argue that the progress is too slow to mitigate the effects of climate change. They believe that unless governments (11) ---- stricter regulations, the corporate world will continue to prioritize profit over sustainability. Nevertheless, the trend (12) ---- to grow as consumers demand more transparency.

The Renaissance was a fervent period of European cultural, artistic, political and economic rebirth following the Middle Ages. Generally described as taking place from the 14th century to the 17th century, the Renaissance promoted the rediscovery of classical philosophy, literature and art. Some of the greatest thinkers, authors, statesmen, scientists and artists in human history thrived during this era, while global exploration opened up new lands and cultures to European commerce. The Renaissance is credited with bridging the gap between the Middle Ages and modern-day civilization.

Graphene is a one-atom-thick layer of carbon atoms arranged in a hexagonal lattice. It is the building block of Graphite, but graphene is a remarkable material on its own with a multitude of astonishing properties. Despite being the thinnest material known to man, it is also about 200 times stronger than steel. Moreover, graphene is an excellent conductor of heat and electricity and has interesting light absorption abilities. It is truly a material that could change the world, with unlimited potential for integration in almost any industry.

questions.question: The archaeological findings provided ---- evidence that the ancient civilization had advanced knowledge of astronomy.

questions.question: While some researchers argue that artificial intelligence will replace human jobs, ----.

options.C: machines are capable of processing data much faster than humans
options.D: the development of AI has been a primary focus of technology companies

options.C: Having been a major industrial hub
options.D: As if the population had decreased

options.C: renewable energy sources are becoming cheaper
options.D: environmentalists have been warning us for years

questions.question: Küresel ısınmanın etkilerini azaltmak için fosil yakıt kullanımını derhal bırakmamız gerekmektedir.

options.A: We should stop using fossil fuels to reduce the effects of global warming.
options.B: In order to mitigate the effects of global warming, we must immediately stop using fossil fuels.
options.C: Global warming effects can only be reduced if we quit fossil fuels right now.
options.D: To decrease global warming, it is necessary that we reduce fossil fuel consumption.
options.E: Stopping the use of fossil fuels is the only way to mitigate global warming.

questions.question: Many people believe that reading books is more beneficial for cognitive development than watching television.

options.A: Birçok insan kitap okumanın bilişsel gelişim için televizyon izlemekten daha faydalı olduğuna inanmaktadır.
options.B: Kitap okumanın televizyondan daha yararlı olduğu birçok kişi tarafından bilinir.
options.C: Televizyon izlemek yerine kitap okumak bilişsel gelişim için daha önemlidir.
options.D: İnsanların çoğu bilişsel gelişim için kitap okumayı televizyona tercih eder.
options.E: Bilişsel gelişim için kitap okumak, televizyon izlemekten daha faydalıdır diye düşünülür.

questions.question: Modern tıp, genetik araştırmalar sayesinde pek çok kalıtsal hastalığın tedavisinde devrim yaratmıştır.

options.A: Modern medicine has revolutionized the treatment of many hereditary diseases thanks to genetic research.
options.B: Genetic research is the reason why modern medicine treats hereditary diseases.
options.C: Many hereditary diseases are being treated by modern medicine and genetic research.
options.D: Through genetic research, many diseases have been cured in modern medicine.
options.E: The revolution in modern medicine is due to the research on hereditary diseases.

questions.question: Although the results of the experiment were inconclusive, they provided valuable insights for future studies.

options.A: Deneyin sonuçları yetersiz olsa da gelecekteki çalışmalar için değerli ipuçları sağladı.
options.B: Deney sonuçsuz kalmasına rağmen, gelecek çalışmalar için değerli bilgiler sundu.
options.C: Sonuçlar kesin olmasa da deney gelecek çalışmalar için çok önemliydi.
options.D: Gelecekteki çalışmalar için değerli olan bu deneyin sonuçları henüz kesinleşmedi.
options.E: Deneyin sonuçları yetersiz olduğu için gelecek çalışmalara ışık tutamadı.

questions.question: It is possible that the flight was cancelled due to the heavy storm.

options.A: The flight must have been cancelled because of the storm.
options.B: The flight might have been cancelled because of the heavy storm.
options.C: The storm definitely caused the flight to be cancelled.
options.D: They should have cancelled the flight because of the storm.
options.E: The flight could not be cancelled despite the storm.

questions.question: You are not allowed to enter the laboratory without wearing a protective suit.

options.B: You can enter the laboratory if you want to wear a suit.
options.C: Wearing a protective suit is optional in the laboratory.
options.D: You should not enter the laboratory even if you have a suit.
options.E: Entering the laboratory is forbidden for everyone.

questions.question: She was so exhausted that she fell asleep as soon as her head touched the pillow.

options.A: She fell asleep quickly because she was very tired.
options.B: She was too tired to fall asleep immediately.
options.C: As soon as she lay down, she felt very exhausted.
options.D: The pillow was so soft that she fell asleep instantly.
options.E: She was extremely tired, so she slept until morning.

options.B: The meeting started after the electricity went out.
options.C: The electricity went out as soon as the meeting began.
options.D: The meeting was interrupted because of the power cut.
options.E: When the electricity went out, the meeting was almost over.

questions.question:
Mark: Have you seen the latest documentary on space exploration? 
Jane: Not yet. Is it worth watching? 
Mark: ---- 
Jane: That sounds interesting. I'll check it out tonight.

options.A: I didn't like it much, actually.
options.B: Absolutely! It provides some amazing footage of Mars.
options.C: I haven't watched it either.
options.D: It is quite long and boring.
options.E: Space exploration is a waste of money.

questions.question:
Teacher: Why haven't you finished your assignment yet? 
Student: ---- 
Teacher: That is no excuse. You should have started earlier.

options.A: I finished it yesterday, sir.
options.B: I found the topic very easy to research.
options.C: I've been dealing with some personal issues lately.
options.D: I really enjoyed doing the project.
options.E: The library was closed for the holidays.

questions.question:
Doctor: How have you been feeling since we changed your medication? 
Patient: Much better, but I still have some headaches in the morning. 
Doctor: ---- 
Patient: Okay, I will try that and let you know.

options.A: That's normal, don't worry about it.
options.B: We might need to adjust the dosage slightly.
options.C: I think you should stop taking the medicine.
options.D: Headaches are not related to this drug.
options.E: You should drink more coffee in the morning.

questions.question:
Alice: Do you think the city's new recycling program will be successful? 
Bob: I'm not sure. It depends on how much the residents participate. 
Alice: ---- 
Bob: Exactly. Education is the key to making it work.

options.A: Most people don't care about the environment.
options.B: The city needs to provide more recycling bins.
options.C: Public awareness campaigns might help increase participation.
options.D: Recycling is too expensive for the municipality.
options.E: I prefer to throw everything in the same bin.

questions.question: Psychology is the scientific study of the mind and behavior. It is a multifaceted discipline and includes many sub-fields of study. ----. In addition, it is closely linked to other fields like biology and sociology.

options.B: It explores topics such as human development, sports, and health
options.C: The history of psychology dates back to ancient Greece
options.D: Mental health is an important part of overall well-being
options.E: Many people find psychology to be a fascinating subject

options.B: Fossil fuels are still the dominant source of energy
options.C: Global warming is a serious threat to our planet
options.D: Nuclear energy is also a low-carbon option
options.E: Governments are providing subsidies for green energy

questions.question: The Great Wall of China is one of the most impressive architectural feats in history. Stretching over 13,000 miles, it was built to protect the Chinese empire from invasions. ----. Today, it stands as a symbol of China's historical strength.

options.B: Many tourists visit the wall every year
options.C: The wall was not built all at once but over several centuries
options.D: It is visible from space according to some myths
options.E: China has a very long and rich history

options.B: Many employees are afraid of losing their jobs to AI
options.C: Cybersecurity is a major concern for digital businesses
options.D: Technology is evolving faster than ever before

questions.question: It was not until the 19th century that scientists began to understand the nature of bacteria.

options.A: Scientists understood bacteria well before the 19th century.
options.B: The 19th century marked the beginning of scientific understanding regarding bacteria.
options.C: Bacteria were only discovered during the 19th century.
options.D: By the end of the 19th century, everything about bacteria was known.
options.E: The nature of bacteria was a mystery until the 20th century.

questions.question: If I had known you were coming, I would have prepared a nice dinner.

options.A: I knew you were coming, so I prepared a dinner.
options.B: I didn't prepare a nice dinner because I didn't know you were coming.
options.C: I am preparing a dinner now because you have arrived.
options.D: You should have told me you were coming for dinner.
options.E: I didn't know you were coming, but I still prepared dinner.

options.C: You should practice every day to speak a language.
options.D: Fluency is not always guaranteed by practice alone.
options.E: If you don't practice, you will never learn a language.

questions.question: (I) Coffee is one of the most popular beverages in the world. (II) It is made from roasted coffee beans. (III) Brazil is the largest producer of coffee globally. (IV) Tea is also a very common drink in many cultures. (V) Many people rely on its caffeine content to stay alert.

questions.question: (I) Mars is often called the Red Planet because of its iron-rich soil. (II) Scientists have been searching for signs of life on Mars for decades. questions.question: According to the passage, the Renaissance is characterized by ----.

options.C: a rebirth of culture and art after the Middle Ages
options.D: the isolation of European commerce from the world
options.E: the end of global exploration and discovery

options.A: there was a lack of significant thinkers and authors
options.B: Europe became more connected to other parts of the world
options.C: science and art were considered less important than politics
options.D: the gap between the Middle Ages and modern times widened

questions.question: What is the main role credited to the Renaissance in the passage?

options.B: Preventing the start of modern-day civilization
options.C: Acting as a bridge between the Middle Ages and the modern era
options.D: Limiting the economic growth of European nations
options.E: Focusing solely on religious developments

questions.question: The passage states that graphene is remarkable because ----.
